# WETC — Wrapped Ether on Ethereum Classic

> WETC is the canonical ERC-20 tokenized version of ETC, enabling DeFi composability on Ethereum Classic. Wrap native ETC into WETC to trade, provide liquidity, and interact with smart contracts.

## About

WETC (Wrapped ETC) is the standard ERC-20 wrapper for native ETC on Ethereum Classic. It follows the same WETH9 pattern used on Ethereum mainnet — a minimal, battle-tested contract that converts native ETC into an ERC-20 token at a 1:1 ratio. This enables ETC to be used in DeFi protocols, decentralized exchanges, and any smart contract expecting ERC-20 tokens.

## How It Works

1. **Wrap** — Send native ETC to the WETC contract. Receive WETC tokens 1:1.
2. **Use** — Trade WETC on DEXs, provide liquidity, use in DeFi protocols.
3. **Unwrap** — Burn WETC tokens. Receive native ETC back 1:1.

## Contract

- Address: `0x1953cab0E5bFa6D4a9BaD6E05fD46C1CC6527a5a`
- Standard: ERC-20 (WETH9 pattern)
- Networks: ETC Mainnet (Chain 61) + Mordor Testnet (Chain 63)
- Source: github.com/wrappedether/canonical-wetc

## Key Features

- 1:1 ETC backing — no minting, no governance, no admin keys
- Immutable contract — no proxy, no upgrades
- Battle-tested WETH9 design — same pattern securing billions on Ethereum
- Listed on CoinGecko and CoinMarketCap

## Ecosystem

- ETCswap — Wrap/unwrap and trade WETC (etcswap.org)
- Classic USD — USD stablecoin using WETC as collateral (classicusd.com)
- Classic OS — Economic command center for ETC (classicos.org)
- Trezor — Hardware wallet for secure ETC/WETC storage

## Web Properties

- wrappedether.org — This site (WETC information and wrapping guide)
- etcswap.org — DEX for trading WETC
- classicusd.com — Stablecoin backed by WETC
- github.com/wrappedether — Source code

## Authors

- Ethereum Classic Community
